evidence: CSWP 39 §3 cites ML-KEM (FIPS 203) as one of the three mandatory-to-implement post-quantum algorithms for systems transitioning away from classical public-key cryptography.
CSWP 39 §3 cites ML-DSA (FIPS 204) as one of the three mandatory-to-implement post-quantum algorithms alongside ML-KEM and SLH-DSA.
CSWP 39 §3 cites SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) as the stateless hash-based signature algorithm required for cryptographic agility and backup-algorithm resilience.

CSWP 39 §4 applies the SP 800-131A Rev 3 transition framework as the normative deprecation schedule for classical algorithm migration; CSWP 39 guidance is a policy application of that broader transition framework.
CSWP 39 §2 references IR 8413 (Third Round Status Report) as the baseline selection rationale for the three standardized PQC algorithms; IR 8413 documents the selection decisions CSWP 39 builds upon.

CSWP 39 §5 references TLS 1.3 (RFC 8446) as the primary network protocol context for PQC migration; hybrid key exchange via ML-KEM is the main deployment path for CSWP 39 recommendations in transport security.

CSWP 39 §5 references KEM integration in CMS (RFC 9629) as the messaging and certificate infrastructure path for PQC migration; organizations implementing CSWP 39 must address CMS-based key transport as well as TLS.
